Once again, please bring any major changes about the sign convention to the discussion page, because many articles on thermodynamics use the minus sign and your proposed change would have to be reflected on many other pages for consistency reasons

/* First Law */ A change this significant must not be done without consulting it first in the discussion page. Many of the articles related to thermodynamics are written with the minus convention in mind, only switching to plus in articles on thermochemistry and physical chemistry

Physics also contributed to many branches of mathematics (i.e. calculus and complesx analysis), biology contributed to organic chemistry, and physics itself led to the development of quantum chemistry. Furthermore, nearly all official definitions of thermodynamics have consistently defined it as a branch of physics. Lastle, chemistry already has [[chemical thermodynamics]] and [[thermochemistry]] for chemical applications of thermodynamics
a broad church! Chemistry did contribute to thermodynamics from the very beginning
